$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for all distribution channel configs. Edit this file → run `npm run generate:configs` → all 9+ artifacts regenerate. CI verifies committed artifacts match source.
name: mnemoverse
title: Mnemoverse Memory
description: Hosted memory for AI agents that learns and forgets — feedback reranks what helps; recall across Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT & any MCP client.

MNEMOVERSE_API_KEY.description: Mnemoverse API key (starts with mk_live_). Optional to install and inspect — the server starts and lists its tools without a key; a key is required only to store or recall memories. Get one free in ~30s at https://console.mnemoverse.com

MNEMOVERSE_API_URL.description: Mnemoverse API base URL. Leave as the default unless you self-host the core engine.

mcpb.$comment: Fields specific to the Claude Desktop Extension (MCPB) manifest.json. version + identity fields are derived from package.json + the source above; only MCPB-specific extras live here.
mcpb.name: mnemoverse-memory
mcpb.longDescription: Mnemoverse gives your AI assistant long-term memory that persists across sessions and across tools. Store preferences, decisions, and lessons with memory_write; recall them by natural-language search with memory_read. The same memory is shared everywhere you use your Mnemoverse API key — Claude, Cursor, VS Code, and any MCP client. Requires a free API key from https://console.mnemoverse.com.

api_key.title: Mnemoverse API Key
api_key.description: Mnemoverse API key (starts with mk_live_). Optional — the server installs and lists its tools without it; required only to store or recall memories. Get one free at https://console.mnemoverse.com

tools.name: memory_write
tools.description: Store a memory that should persist across sessions and tools — preferences, decisions, lessons, project facts.

tools.name: memory_read
tools.description: Search your long-term memory by natural-language query before answering or starting a task.

tools.name: memory_feedback
tools.description: Report whether a recalled memory was helpful so good memories surface faster next time.

tools.name: memory_stats
tools.description: Show how many memories are stored, which domains exist, and average quality scores.

tools.name: memory_delete
tools.description: Permanently delete a single memory by its atom_id.

tools.name: memory_delete_domain
tools.description: Permanently delete every memory in a domain (requires an explicit confirm).
